label: Sea Lamprey Threat and Management Efforts

comments.text: Dark skies reflect my sadness at the firings of all the humans that help keep the #GreatLakes as healthy as possible. Tonight I mourn the loss of the 14 .bsky.social employees that kept the invasive sea lamprey at bay. My fishery will be hurt. 😢😢🌊🌊

Sweeping layoffs of federal employees have struck the program responsible for controlling the invasive sea lamprey that threatens fish across the Great Lakes, the earth’s largest freshwater ecosystem.

Yesterday, I saw firsthand how invasive and downright diabolical sea lampreys are — and what will happen to our Great Lakes if we stop work on this invasive species. To demonstrate how they they swarm, the scientists had me spit into a barrel of these suckers — and you can see the results yourself.

There are 4 native lamprey species in the #GreatLakes (chestnut, silver, American brook + northern brook) and 1 invasive (sea lamprey). Chestnut lamprey is parasitic like the sea lamprey, but they cause less harm because of their much smaller size + co-evolution with native fish #25DaysofFishmas
The lamprey is a very scary aquatic animal and I’m fully shook to have learned that they live in the Great Lakes. I thought those vampiric fuckers were deep sea dwellers.
With everything going on, it’s good to know that a Senator from Michigan and a Senator from Ohio agree that we must do everything we can to keep the Sea Lamprey out of our Great Lakes.

Okay correct me if I’m wrong, but this BBC Wildlife illustration accompanying an article about unisexual Great Lakes mole salamanders sure looks like a Spotted Sandpiper (Ambystoma maculatum), notably not one of the five (5!!) species’ genomes present in the hybrid unisex Great Lakes populations.

🎶Chestnuts roasting on an open fire🎶 As another cool native #GreatLakes species often mistaken for being invasive, the Chestnut Lamprey (Ichthyomyzon castaneus) is perfect for today’s #GarWeek theme of “Invasive Species Awareness”! Though they may not be winning any beauty contests #25DaysofFishmas

New research shows that COVID-19 disruptions of lampricide applications has led to increased adult invasive sea lamprey abundance across the #GreatLakes basin. Consistent treatment is critically important + reductions in suppression can quickly lead to sea lamprey resurgence doi.org/10.1093/fshm...
A yellow collared scape moth (Cisseps fulvicollis) feeds from goldenrod along the shores of Lake Michigan.

Not much is scarier for a #GreatLakes fish than the invasive sea lamprey, sometimes called vampire fish 🧛 But while they're more trick than treat in their introduced range, they're actually threatened in parts of their native range of the Atlantic coasts of Europe + North America - that's scary!

That will collapse the great lakes fisheries. The invasive sea lamprey (Petromyzon marinus) that was responsible for the collapse during the 1940-1960's is only controlled through joint efforts of both nations and these agreements. What a damned moron.
Sea lamprey for #SundayFishSketch. Learned about potential genetic biocontrol in the #GreatLakes last week.

Wiping out the entire USFWS invasive lamprey team in Michigan is going to end up ruining the multi-billion dollar Great Lakes commercial fishery. 14 people making about 32K a year, we’re single handily keeping the lamprey at bay. And now they’re fired. Trump is an idiot

It’s time to do AWAY with the LAMPREYS. They’re killing our fish and decimating our ecosystems. That’s why we’re celebrating $2.1 million in grant dollars secured for the Great Lakes Fishery Commission to control sea lamprey in Lake Michigan–right here in Grand Rapids.

Unlike the Sea Lamprey (Petromyzon marinus) which is invasive in the #GreatLakes, Ohio lamprey have evolved with host fishes in their native range + rarely kill them. Ohio lamprey are also much smaller than sea lamprey (max length = 15 in for Ohio lamprey vs. 47 in for sea lamprey) #25DaysofFishmas

Proper maintenance of our Great Lakes is essential to our economy and environment, which includes controlling invasive species like the sea lamprey. The Trump Admin’s widespread firing of USFWS employees tasked with lamprey control efforts is highly concerning to communities along the Great Lakes.🧵

Here's the weird COVID news for the week. It turns out that populations of the invasive Sea Lamprey in the Great Lakes are rebounding in response to eradication efforts pausing during the pandemic. Populations of their favorite prey, Lake Trout, rebounded in recent years but are now at risk. 🌎🌿🧪

Our Great Lakes coalition made WAVES. When the Trump Administration and DOGE slashed jobs at USFWS, I joined with Rep. Dingell and called out how dangerous this would be for the Great Lakes as we fight the invasive sea lamprey. It worked.
Lake trout were nearly wiped out in Lake Superior by the invasive sea lamprey. But a successful control project has helped bring the trout back from the brink.

Surveys of the lamprey show their numbers have returned to pre-pandemic levels, according to a report from the Great Lakes Fishery Commission. Story by .bsky.social:
www.greatlakesnow.org/2026/01/12/i...

New poster alert! We just released "Lampreys of the Great Lakes" with the Great Lakes Fishery Commission, available now in our store. And now is the perfect time to order with our holiday discount (**get 20% off your order with promo code HOLIDAY20 through December 18**)! buff.ly/PLDvGnK

Officials celebrate the near-eradication of invasive creature in Great Lakes: 'This is an unprecedented victory' After more than a century of devastation, officials in the Great Lakes have declared victory over the invasive sea lamprey. www.thecooldown.com/outdoors/sea...

It's National Invasive Species Awareness Week. Learn more about control efforts for sea lampreys, which now face setbacks as federal staff cuts threaten progress — despite the Great Lakes Fishery Commission’s recent announcement of lake trout’s full rebound: www.greatlakesnow.org/2025/02/lake...
Dear Great Lakes Fishermen: $200m represents ten years of funding to fight the invasive Sea Lamprey saving our Lake Trout and Salmon. But DOGE cut it out and you got this bullshit ad instead.

“I always tell people that if I ever feel like our funding is in doubt I’ll just come with big tanks of sea lamprey and release them in their offices in Congress or in Parliament.” Ethan Baker, chair, Great Lakes Fishery Commission that works to suppress the population of invasive sea lampreys 🧪🌎
What's up with Sea Lamprey in the #GreatLakes? I wrote about it in the current issue of Inland Seas!
Speaking as a biologist from the Great Lakes where we have lots of invasive sea lamprey, it's a fun party trick to stick them on you (regardless of political party).

The sea lamprey have no natural predators in the Great Lakes. Scientists figured out a chemical that stopped their breeding in the 1950s, but didn’t hurt other species. Now we are stopping the scientists. insideclimatenews.org/news/2702202...

For decades, sea lamprey have sucked their way through the Great Lakes, crashing ecosystems, killing native fish, while costing Great Lakes communities Millions. Last week, educational sea lamprey made their way to the House for a special Great Lakes Task Force event during Capitol Hill Oceans Week.
Good morning, friends! I think I saw a sea lamprey yesterday when I was down by the river. They’re an invasive species in the Great Lakes, and they do get into the rivers and streams.

CONGRATULATIONS to Alex Duncan, who passed his #PhD defense "Ginebigomeg (Petromyzon marinus): Indigenous Governance, Sea Lamprey, and the Politics of Aquatic Invasion in the Great Lakes" with 'flying colours". Hooray for Dr. Duncan!

Invasive sea lamprey control isn't a done deal in the Great Lakes. As the Great Lakes Fishery Commission recently announced, sea lamprey pops briefly spiked after pandemic travel restrictions interrupted control teams in 2020-21: (T. Lawrence, Great Lakes Fishery Commission)
The lampricide 3-trifluoromethyl-4-nitrophenol (TFM) stimulates oxygen consumption by larval sea lamprey in a dose-dependent manner #GreatLakes 🧪 www.sciencedirect.com/science/arti...

I’m working a blog about lamprey, the Great Lakes, and invasive species for Fish History (fishhistory.substack.com) What questions do people have about lamprey, or cool piece of information you’d like to see included? I’m especially interested in what you’d like to know about them. 🧪

2-for-1 special! A few weeks ago, we caught a lake sturgeon with a bonus silver lamprey attached to it. Unlike their invasive cousins (sea lamprey) in the Great Lakes, this native species evolved to live in balance with various host species. #52FishyFridays 🐟🐠🌎

After a heavy few months, it felt refreshing to host the
Great Lakes Commission today, who shared insights about invasive Sea Lamprey and their bi-national role in protecting the Great Lakes and fisheries. 🌎🐟 The students were thrilled to get hands-on with the lamprey and everyone was smiling.

Invasive Sea Lamprey get a lot of attention, but it's super cool that there are native freshwater lamprey in the Laurentian Great Lakes basin! Not super cool that the native lamprey face significant threats to their existence.
Larger larval sea lamprey (Petromyzon marinus) have longer survival times when exposed to the lampricide 3-trifluoromethyl-4-nitrophenol #GreatLakes 🧪 www.sciencedirect.com/science/arti...

Unfortunately, invasive Sea Lamprey populations grew after covid limited management efforts. This year, they were detected for the first time in the East Arm of the Little Calumet River – my tiny, beleaguered watershed! 💀 (Citation links in AltText).

Want to fish in the Great Lakes? Then stop invasive sea lamprey. The good news is we know how - people wade into the creeks/rivers where lampreys breed to spray a chemical that kills lamprey, but not other species. The $20 million program protects the multi-billion $ fishery.

Latest #Fisheries: Great Lakes Invasive species distribution models (open access), Control reduction during pandemic corresponds to Sea Lamprey increase, The fuzzy boundary around commercial, subsistence, and recreational fisheries (open access), and more: academic.oup.com/fisheries/is...

They're only invasive in some areas of the states (the great lakes specifically)! They're indigenous to other areas, including pretty much the entirety of both coasts. Here in the PNW, the pacific lamprey is actually a protected species!
Oh my gosh, sea lamprey! Ehhhhbbbhhhh! I am so sorry, Lake Michigan. Our beloved Gulf of Mexico has also been having a difficult time of it. But remember, water is stronger.

While we are doing our best to keep sea lamprey under control in the #GreatLakes, I always keep in mind that they are a welcome (and struggling) critter back home. 🇨🇦🇵🇹 Can your research on an invasive species contribute towards its preservation in its native range? Always an interesting thought.

They call the sea lamprey the #vampire of the Great Lakes! Look at the mouth on those things! You can learn about them and many other other-worldly creatures in my gothic #horror, The Dance of Rotten Sticks. 💙📚 #booksky Just $2.99 on #kindle www.amazon.com/Dance-Rotten...

The sea lamprey controls also affect the 4 native species in the Great Lakes. The chestnut and silver lamprey are parasitic in their adult stage but do not kill the host. The American brook and the northern brook lamprey are not parasitic. www.cbc.ca/news/canada/...

This collaborative research explores Indigenous perspectives on Bimiizii (sea lamprey), an invasive species introduced to the Great Lakes in the mid-1800s. This project will also emphasize relational understandings often absent in mainstream invasion ecology. Way to go, Beth and team!

Join the Northeast-Midwest Institute and the Great Lakes Fishery Commission on Wednesday, June 10th, from 2:00 - 3:00 p.m. in Cannon House Office Building 330 to learn about the invasive sea lamprey in the Great Lakes. Hold a live sea lamprey! Learn more about GLFC's efforts: glfc.org/control.php

Ash goes exclusively by, well, Ash, but her legal name is Ashley. Sebastian is quite famous as the “Lake Huron Lion”, a new cryptid caught on camera by a hiker… though when he’s first introduced, nobody else has linked that accidental “identity” to him.
Seasonal differences in larval sea lamprey (Petromyzon marinus) sensitivity to the pesticide TFM #GreatLakes doi.org/10.1016/j.jg...
Acute toxicity of lampricides to non-target species of concern in the Lake Champlain watershed www.sciencedirect.com/science/arti... #GreatLakes

Meet the invasive #SeaLamprey, a parasitic fish that has been harming the #GreatLakes for decades – and learn how international cooperation and technological breakthroughs protect native fish from this aquatic vampire. #NISAW

A lindwurm is a type of salamander from the proteidae family. It can measure up to 6ft long and it lives in caves in great lakes. It scours lakes for food using primarily its barbels. Although it’s also known for having better eyes than its cousins as well. Pt 1. Tags: spec evo, speculative

Highly invasive sea lamprey's nearly wiped out the native Lake Trout fishery on the Great Lakes. Our resource departments put lampricide in streams all around the Great Lakes and yes, it is illegal to possess sea lampreys in the United States without a permit.
We've finally found the way to get rid of sea lamprey in the great lakes. Turn them into purses! 🤭 That looks pretty cool!

wgntv.com/morning-news...
Sun-Times reporter Bob Herguth joins us to talk about the sea lamprey, an invasive species of the Great Lakes that was significantly reduced by scientists, but now it’s feared they may surge.

(Also, damnit, RFK Jr. actually got me partially feeling sorry for an invasive species--poor lampreys got imported to the Great Lakes by accident by shipping through the St. Lawrence Seaway, they sure as hell didn't ask to be used as a giant leech substitute for "bleeding" by RFK Jr)
The Great Lakes Fishery Commission has announce their call for proposals for funding year 2027 projects on fisheries & sea lamprey control www.glfc.org/call-for-pro...
I just imagine the DOGEr staggering out of Lake Michigan with a sea lamprey attached to his big balls.
All we need to do is figure out how to deliver vaccines via lamprey bite, and RFK jr will approve of them again. Someone get Lake Michigan on the phone.

Wait---I thought I've been a part of your lab since I saw you in 2018?? I've been studying Lake Ontario sawfish the whole time. It's not easy to find specimens so I'm running behind with my paper www.instagram.com/p/BlqHwyGAbqJ/

Really, really awesome documentary. I once found a native lamprey nest in the Eau Claire River in Wausau, WI. They are freaky. Never have seen an alewife. #salmonfever #sealamprey #greatlakes #lakesuperior #TFM #laketrout #science #alewife #alewives #waterscience youtu.be/lhnif6AjXx8?...

I had a guy once compare the sea Lamprey to wolves. An invasive species that caused massive devastation to the fish populations in the great lakes to a species that is native to the region and lost most of its native range because of cattle death numbers being widely exaggerated
Caught a fish with a lamprey attached while out with my daughter I have seen sea lamprey out in the Great Lakes but this is an interior lake in Michigan. Had no idea we had native lamprey until …

No, sir, I watched an excellent documentary on the sea lamprey invasion at the Great Lakes, a terrible situation that requires the cooperation of both 🇨🇦 & 🇺🇸so the fishing industry isn’t destroyed. It got me to thinking the junta in 🇺🇸 is a lot like that lamprey, invasive and destructive! Ugly too

GlobalNews.ca: "A parasitic invasive species threatens Great Lakes fish populations, $7B industry" The Great Lakes Fishery Commission has announced the annual sea lamprey abundances are above population targets in all five of the Great Lakes. …

The Great Lakes Fishery Commission is set to begin its sea lamprey control program at the end of April after pauses and delays caused by potential funding and staffing cuts. The program protects the $5 billion fishing industry from this invasive species. Read more at:
Also staff in charge of program to limit lamprey populations in the Great Lakes have been laid off, by the time people realize what’s going on, it will be too late

No doubt. Our Great Lakes lamprey are invasive, but I've stuck them on my hand and arm before. But not trying to leave marks, just as a demo.

The Northeast-Midwest Institute and the Great Lakes Fishery Commission co-hosted a live sea lamprey encounter on Capitol Hill last week. Nearly 80 people attended from 25 Congressional offices and committees. Read more about the event here: buff.ly/9K3q4XG

Naegleria fowleri is found in lots of freshwater, but probably not super prevalent in the great lakes. There's a reported case in MI, but they mostly thrive in the sediment of warm water. The absolute highest risk is in water that's been recently disturbed with kicked up dirt

The Northeast-Midwest Institute and the Great Lakes Fishery Commission hosted a live sea lamprey event on Capitol Hill. Attendees held sea lampreys and learned how the GLFC has reduced sea lamprey populations, preserving the $5.1 billion Great Lakes fishery. Read more: www.nemw.org/lamprey-even...

Another thing I learned is that sea lamprey are not just edible but palatable to humans as well. Though I would recommend if you live near the Great Lakes maybe not trying them. Because if you know, the mercury.

I walked through a web out walking by Lake Michigan this morning and noticed the spider who spun it trailing after me on a thread stuck to my clothes. It spun itself to the ground and trotted away. Have to imagine that kind of thing happens often to webspinning spiders, they must be a patient sort.

Hundreds of chad washed up on a beach on Lake Erie, Canada side, about a week ago. I am curious why no creatures have eaten them, like seagulls? They're still there. My dog won't touch them, either, and, trust me, this dog eats anything. Thanks, Science.

My dad fished the Great Lakes for 75 years. I have seen a lamprey on a lake trout and it a legitimate nightmare. Those invasive species including the bighead carp need to be eliminated. A lot of sport fishermen are conservative. Maybe this will change their minds.
Uh, the Great Lakes Sea Lamprey Program is to kill them (they're an invasive species).

Just watched a doc about the sea lamprey in the Great Lakes on Prime, very timely. Effort has been going on for nearly 100 years. Unfortunately restoring streams for certain fish species also creates spawning ground for lamprey.

Efforts to curb the invasive Sea Lamprey in the Great Lakes will resume after the Trump administration reversed staffing cuts. The lampricide program has been delayed, however, and will be behind schedule. For the full story, see:

there are invasive lampreys in the great lakes, but the pacific lamprey is native to where i live & a sacred food source for indigenous people. tribal leaders from yakama, umatilla, nez perce, & warm springs come together every year to put on the willamette falls lamprey celebration. it's wonderful.

$423 billion every year. That is the collective cost of invasive species. We saw this in Michigan and Ohio with emerald ash borer wiping out all five species of our native ash trees. FYI - Sea lamprey infestations can threaten up to $5B in annual Great Lakes fishery value. Losses can exceed $100M a year, harming jobs, recreation, and food supply—devastation comparable to a major crop pest disaster. Understand the importance now?

The Great Lakes Fishery Commission, an international organization established by the United States and Canada through the 1954 Convention on Great Lakes Fisheries, manages and controls the invasive sea lamprey, which has threatened the ecosystem for decades. www.thefishthief.com
